channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Flytipping on the public pavement on Balmes Road., 24th June
item.description:
Flytipping on the public pavement on Balmes Road.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Balmes Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Major refurbishment of heritage postbox on corner of Ravenstone road, 24th June
item.description:
This relatively rare king Edward vii heritage postbox has been slowly decaying and been neglected for years. It must rank amongst the worst in London? And compares woefully to others in the rest of Britain. Royal Mail probably think that why should they bother given the state of everything else around here so they can hide from their civic responsibilities. It needs straightening up in the vertical plane, removal of the bolted on stash box to the side that is no longer operationally used and that has become a graffiti board and a brand new paint job. I have reported it to Royal Mail and followed up…to no avail. These stakeholders in our community need mot accountability, it simply not acceptable but they seem to be able to do what they want?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 2, Ravenstone Road, Hornsey, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Racist / religious graffiti on billboards next to Boucher primary school on grange road, 24th June
item.description:
Islamic related religious graffiti on billboards next to Boucher primary School (only half the amount was removed and then council closed the case, so I am now re-reporting the rest of it!)

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Grange Road, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Awful Toilet Facilities, 24th June
item.description:
The toilet facilities in Kelsey Park are in a VERY poor state and on many occasions there is no hand soap available for users. This is unhygienic and creates a poor experience for visitors, particularly families with young children.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Stone Park Avenue, Eden Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tent pitched on the footpath, 24th June
item.description:
A tent has been pitched on the footpath since Monday 22nd June. It looks like someone might be living in it. Concerned for their health and safety in the hot weather. Also concerned for the safety of passer-by's, which include school aged children.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cricket Lane, New Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: The dead fox is located in front of the football cage on Lorrimore Square near Carter Street, 24th June
item.description:
There is a dead fox in front of the football/basketball cage at Lorrimore Square in front of St Paul's church. It needs to be removed urgently as it's a very hot day and could attract flies especially as children and families want to play in the football cage

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lorrimore Square, Elephant and Castle, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bagged dead baby fox for disposal, 24th June
item.description:
The fox is dead, we have double bagged it and put out of the sun. On Kent house Road by round about with parish lane and Kings Hall. It’s actually between side of 1 kings hall and 293 Kent house in blue thick garden bag.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Kent House Road, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter, 24th June
item.description:
Litter pick needed on pathway between Station Approach and William Foster Lane. William Foster Lane also needs a litter pick. Job was closed as completed, this isn't the case.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): William Foster Lane, Welling, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Trip hazard, 24th June
item.description:
Massive trip hazard - new bitumen installed over new grating

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Charlton Church Lane, Charlton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Items left outside 41 marcia road, 24th June
item.description:
Items are left outside 41 Marcia road

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Marcia Road, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Broken pavement and drainage, 24th June
item.description:
The drain has snapped and it’s leaking with water. The pavement is also broken and lifts when it’s stepped on (making more water come out). This is making our drive way unusable and therefore causing us to use unnecessary space on the road

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Vaughan Road, East Wickham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Engine idling red discovery, 24th June
item.description:
Engine idling for last 20 minutes by the bins super loud and polluting car
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: This is a Council problem as this is a public road not a private road oistide Lakeview/ Hillview, 24th June
item.description:
Reported yesterday and told lt wasn't a council problem. It is a public road with houses that could have flooding in their basements if drains stay blocked. This has been dealt with by the council before

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 12, Vale of Health, Belsize Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish has been thrown out of a car in the car park, including food waste, 24th June
item.description:
Rubbish has been thrown out of a car and is littering the car parking area of Wateringbury Close

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 20, Wateringbury Close, St Paul's Cray, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead fox, 24th June
item.description:
A fox was run over on the road, an operative from a recycling truck dragged the dead fox on to the pavement in front of number 9 springfield gardens. It's covered with an old jacket but there are flies all over it and it's starting to smell.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Springfield Gardens, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Discarded fridge on the public footpath outside 24 Groombridge Road., 24th June
item.description:
Discarded fridge on the public footpath outside 24 Groombridge Road.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Groombridge Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: London Fire Brigade attended e-battery on fire at Trevithick House, 24th June
item.description:
The Emergency Planning & Resilience Officer at Southwark Council became aware of this battery that had been thrown from 9th floor whilst on fire, after receiving a report from London Fire Brigade. Emergency Planning staff member had advised Street Cleansing Team, who arranged for safe disposal of the battery 07:00 24th June after the battery had been submerged for over 12 hours in water, as per the usual LFB advise. Lime Bikes will additionally be notified, so they can track their e-PLV - no information available whether there is a lime bike at the premises.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Gallewall Trading Estate, Bermondsey, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Discarded mattress and bed frame on the public footpath outside 71 Cassland Road., 24th June
item.description:
Discarded mattress and bed frame on the public footpath outside 71 Cassland Road.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Cassland Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:

item.title: Taken over the gardens, 24th June
item.description:
No sun always leaves to get rid of it needs pruning

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Adams Road, Upper Elmers End, Greater London
